behaviors.description: Primary translator output is grammatically coherent and preserves source meaning (not literal word substitution).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real neural translation model quality, not just deterministic wiring. Mocking the model would hide real failures in fluency/meaning preservation. This is a single agentic step, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run a fixed multilingual evaluation set through the real translation API and score each output for semantic equivalence and grammaticality (human rubric or validated semantic-quality judge). Fail if quality thresholds are not met, even when API call succeeds.

behaviors.description: On translation API 503, service transparently falls back to dictionary translator and does not return an error.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is interaction between API client error handling and fallback module selection. A unit test of one function is insufficient; wiring between modules must be exercised. Real external network is not required if 503 is injected at the client boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service with a test API client stub that returns HTTP 503 and a real/in-memory SQLite fallback dictionary. Assert response is successful, contains fallback translation, and no error is propagated to caller.

behaviors.description: Dictionary fallback returns correct pre-stored translation for phrases present in SQLite.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on SQLite read path. Real failures can occur in SQL/query/schema/collation wiring that mocks would miss. No subprocess or external API is required.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a test SQLite DB with known phrase->translation rows, call fallback translator through its repository/service boundary, and assert exact returned translation for existing phrases.

behaviors.description: Quality tracking computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: This behavior spans computation plus persistence. Unit tests can verify distance math, but real failures include incorrect storage mapping/transaction wiring, which require module integration with DB.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke quality-tracking path with known primary/fallback strings and a test SQLite metrics store; assert persisted record contains expected edit-distance value and linkage to request/translation IDs.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, service returns primary translation and logs quality comparison asynchronously.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in orchestration across translator selection and background quality-logging components. It does not require validating model quality itself, only control-flow and async side effects. Mocking all boundaries at unit level could miss scheduler/queue wiring defects.
behaviors.test_description: Run service with both translators enabled (primary stub success + SQLite fallback), call translate, assert returned text is from primary immediately, then await background task completion and assert quality-comparison log/metric was written.
